title: neon-noir: unify hover glow color to match accent-hover
description: The neon-noir hover glow mixes two hardcoded colors (cyan + magenta rgba). Tom wants the glow to use only the accent-hover color (#ff2d95) so the glow matches the highlight color consistently.

features: In src/themes/neon-noir.css, add a new custom property to :root: --accent-hover-rgb: 255, 45, 149; (the RGB components of #ff2d95, for use in rgba())
Change the at-rest box-shadow on .links__anchor (line ~84) from 'box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(0, 229, 255, 0.3)' to 'box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(var(--accent-hover-rgb), 0.2)'
Change the at-rest box-shadow on .links__item or .links__anchor base state (line ~129) from 'box-shadow: 0 0 4px rgba(0, 229, 255, 0.1)' to 'box-shadow: 0 0 4px rgba(var(--accent-hover-rgb), 0.1)'

Change the hover box-shadow (line ~137) from 'box-shadow: 0 0 12px var(--accent-color), 0 0 24px rgba(255, 45, 149, 0.15)' to 'box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(var(--accent-hover-rgb), 0.6), 0 0 24px rgba(var(--accent-hover-rgb), 0.2)'
Update the comment at the top of the file (line 3) from 'Near-black background with neon cyan and magenta accents.' to 'Near-black background with neon cyan accent and magenta highlight glow.'

notes: CSS custom properties can't be used directly inside rgba() in older syntax, but rgba(var(--accent-hover-rgb), 0.6) works in all modern browsers. The accent-hover-rgb variable holds just the comma-separated RGB values: '255, 45, 149'. The accent-hover hex (#ff2d95) stays as-is for the border-color and text color on hover.
